skill.name: grill-me
skill.description: Guides an AI assistant to question a plan or design until decisions, dependencies, and assumptions are clear.
skill.summary: Stress-test plans and designs through structured, one-question-at-a-time review.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported four high-severity weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md, but the referenced lines contain natural language instructions only. No cryptographic code, scripts, network access, file writes, secret access, external commands, or prompt-injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:6 as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. Both locations contain prose describing an interview workflow, not code or a cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain-language instructions and contain no executable code, algorithm names, libraries, or credential handling. The file has only one instruction document, so the finding is strongly supported as a false positive.

content.user_title: Stress Test Plans With Relentless Questions
content.value_statement: Plans often fail because assumptions stay hidden until implementation starts. This skill turns Claude, Codex, or Claude Code into a focused reviewer that asks one question at a time.
actual_capabilities: Asks targeted questions about a plan or design.
Works through decisions one at a time.
Provides a recommended answer for each question.
Can inspect a codebase when a question can be answered from files.
limitations: Does not implement the plan by itself.
Depends on the user or repository for accurate context.
May require several exchanges for complex designs.
Does not replace expert review for legal, security, or financial decisions.

use_cases.title: Validate a Feature Plan
use_cases.description: Find missing requirements and unclear decisions before development begins.

use_cases.title: Review a Technical Design
use_cases.description: Test architecture choices, dependencies, and tradeoffs before coding.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a Planning Meeting
use_cases.description: Practice answering hard questions before presenting a proposal.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Basic Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill me on this plan. Ask one question at a time and include your recommended answer after each question.

prompt_templates.title: Find Missing Requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill me on these requirements. Focus on ambiguity, missing users, dependencies, and decisions that block implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Stress Test a Technical Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill me on this technical design. Explore the codebase where useful, then ask one unresolved question at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve a Decision Tree
prompt_templates.prompt: Grill me on this proposal until every major branch of the decision tree is resolved. Track dependencies between decisions.

output_examples.input: Grill me on my plan to add team invitations.
output_examples.output: First question: Who can invite users, and does that permission differ by role? Recommended answer: limit invitations to workspace owners and admins unless a clear delegated role exists.

output_examples.input: Review my migration plan before I start coding.
output_examples.output: First question: What is the rollback path if the migration succeeds for some records but fails for others? Recommended answer: define a reversible migration and a verified backup checkpoint.
First question: Which user segment has the strongest need for this workflow? Recommended answer: choose one primary segment before defining scope.

best_practices: Provide the plan, goals, constraints, and known risks at the start.
Answer each question before moving to the next branch.
Let the assistant inspect relevant repository files when code context matters.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you only need a quick approval.
Do not skip questions that expose uncomfortable tradeoffs.
Do not treat recommendations as final without checking project context.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It makes the assistant question a plan or design until key decisions and assumptions are clear.

faq.question: Does it ask all questions at once?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the assistant to ask one question at a time.

faq.question: Can it review code context?
faq.answer: Yes. If a question can be answered by exploring the codebase, the assistant should inspect the codebase.

faq.question: Is this only for software projects?
faq.answer: No. It can review product, process, and design plans, but it is especially useful for technical planning.

faq.question: Will it make final decisions for me?
faq.answer: It provides recommended answers, but the user remains responsible for final decisions.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No. The skill file contains instruction text only and does not include scripts or executable code.
